title: wolftpm dual API surface: standalone + tpm-rs/TSS
description:
Implement two complementary API surfaces for wolftpm, mirroring how wolfcrypt does both RustCrypto traits and its own API surface.

## Why
wolftpm-sys has raw FFI bindings but no safe Rust API. Two surfaces are needed:
1. Standalone safe API (wraps wolfTPM2_* functions directly)
2. tpm-rs/TSS compatibility (implements tpm2_rs_client::Connection so wolfTPM becomes a backend for any code built on the tpm-rs stack)

## Crate structure
wolftpm-sys
  ├── wolftpm           Standalone wolfTPM high-level API (Device, Key, Error)
  └── wolftpm-tss       tpm-rs Connection impl via wolfTPM transport layer

## Key design decisions
- wolftpm-tss calls TPM2_CTX::ioCb directly (only raw I/O interface in wolfTPM)
- Response length read from bytes 2-5 of TPM2 response header (big-endian, per TCG spec)
- tpm2-rs-client = 0.1.0 pinned (self-described unstable)
- WOLFTPM2_NO_WOLFCRYPT kept in wolftpm-sys; wolftpm-tss uses full bindings
- Phase 1 scope: Device + get_random + pcr_read + ECC P-256 sign/verify
notes: tpm-rs maintainers: Microsoft, Google, Huawei engineers — TCG charter project at github.com/tpm-rs/tpm-rs. Connection trait: transact(cmd: &[u8], rsp: &mut [u8]) -> Result<&mut [u8], Error>. TPM2 response header size field at bytes 2-5 (big-endian u32).
